cards.title: Babylonians & Lloyds
cards.content:
ΓÇó Babylonians (2100 BC): First form of insurance where traders were forgiven loans if shipments were lost.
ΓÇó Lloyds Coffee House (1688): Started in London, became the world's first modern insurance market.

cards.title: Risk Management Techniques
cards.content:
ΓÇó Risk Retention: Bearing the risk yourself (e.g., small deductibles).
ΓÇó Risk Avoidance: Refusing to take the risk (e.g., not flying).
ΓÇó Risk Transfer: Transferring the financial loss to an insurer (This is Insurance).

items.text: Oriental Life Insurance Company: The first life insurance company in India.
Insurance Act 1938: The primary legislation governing insurance in India.
Nationalization of 245 life insurers into a single entity: LIC.
IRDA Act passed, establishing the regulatory authority for insurance.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Insurance works on the "Law of Large Numbers" - predicting the losses of a large group is easier than an individual.
It spreads the loss of a few among many (Mutuality / Pooling of risk).
It does NOT prevent the risk from happening; it only compensates for the financial loss.
Primary function of insurance is "Risk Transfer" and providing certainty.
An asset must have an economic value to be insured.
